sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Levante UD have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, including an impressive 4-0 away win against Girona. Rayo Vallecano, on the other hand, have struggled slightly more, managing 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses in their recent outings, with a narrow 1-0 victory over Real Sociedad as a highlight.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Levante UD have had the upper hand, securing a 4-2 victory in their most recent match against Rayo Vallecano in 2022, while the earlier game in 2021 ended in a 1-1 draw. Historical trends suggest both teams tend to score, with 5 of their last 5 head-to-head matches seeing goals from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Levante UD (13th, 8 points) and Rayo Vallecano (14th, 8 points) are closely matched in the league table. Levante have scored 13 goals this season (7th in the league) but struggle defensively with 14 conceded (15th). Rayo Vallecano have a more balanced but less potent attack with 8 goals scored and 10 conceded.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Levante’s Etta Eyong has been in fine form, netting 4 goals in the last 5 matches, and could be a game-changer. Rayo Vallecano will need to leverage their slightly better defensive structure and late-game scoring tendency (40% of goals in the 76-90th minute period) to counter Levante’s attacking threat.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (43% home win, 25% draw, 32% away win) and Levante’s recent head-to-head dominance, they hold a slight edge at home. However, Rayo’s resilience in tight matches could make this a closely contested game, likely to see goals on both ends based on past trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Rayo Vallecano leads Levante UD 2-0 at the Ciudad de Valencia. The away side has been clinical in the first half, with both goals coming from Jorge de Frutos in the 12th and 25th minutes. Levante UD, despite showing effort, has struggled to convert their opportunities into goals. The home team will need a significant shift in performance to mount a comeback in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano has demonstrated superior attacking efficiency, capitalizing on their big chances created and converting them into goals. Their expected goals reflect a strong likelihood of scoring, which they’ve delivered on. Levante UD, on the other hand, has had a presence in the final third with notable entries and penalty area incursions, but their inability to create big chances or test the goalkeeper consistently has left them trailing. Both teams have shared possession evenly, indicating that the game isn’t dominated by one side in terms of control, but rather in finishing.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: Jorge de Frutos has been the standout performer for Rayo Vallecano, finding the back of the net twice in the first half and posing a constant threat to Levante’s defense. His sharpness in front of goal has been pivotal. For Levante UD, players like Unai Vencedor have shown promise with key contributions in midfield, but the attacking players need to step up. Etta Eyong has had a shot on target, which offers some hope for a potential spark in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Early on, there was a balanced exchange of attacking spells, with Levante UD showing some intent. However, Rayo Vallecano gained a significant edge around the times of their goals, with sustained pressure leading to breakthroughs. Towards the latter stages of the half, Levante UD pushed back with several attacking phases, particularly in the closing minutes, suggesting they might carry some momentum into the second half if they can maintain that intensity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Defensive observations
halftimeSections.text: Defensively, Rayo Vallecano has been solid, with players like Nobel Mendy and Óscar Valentín making crucial clearances and tackles to thwart Levante’s advances. Levante’s defense, despite some interceptions and clearances, has been vulnerable, especially during key moments of pressure from Rayo. This disparity in defensive resilience could be a deciding factor in the second half if Levante cannot tighten up at the back.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead, Levante UD is expected to come out with renewed energy, likely pushing for an early goal to get back into the game. Their attacking efforts in the final minutes of the first half could be a sign of intent, and they may focus on creating more direct chances. Rayo Vallecano, with a comfortable lead, might adopt a slightly more conservative approach to protect their advantage but will remain dangerous on the counter with de Frutos leading the charge. A key area to watch will be whether Levante can capitalize on their final third presence to test Rayo’s defense more effectively.
